---
title: "AutoProxy as a communication intelligence layer, not a messaging app"
description: "The architecture implication of the single-pane vision: ingest from multiple channels, enrich with PMS data, route/filter/summarize, present per role"
date: 2026-03-14
group: zk
type: note
id: "20260314-single-pane-communication-intelligence-layer"
links:
  - "20260314-pms-integration-as-structural-moat"
  - "20260314-corporate-property-communication-fault-line"
  - "20260314-report-intelligence-highest-value-corporate-wedge"
  - "20260314-two-scopes-one-pipeline"
source: "projects/autoproxy/MARKET_RESEARCH_CORPORATE"
created_by: agent
---

The corporate market research reveals that AutoProxy's most defensible position is not as a messaging app but as a communication intelligence layer. The distinction has profound architectural implications.

A messaging app competes on channels, UX, and network effects -- a losing battle against Teams and Slack. A communication intelligence layer sits on top of all existing channels and provides: (1) unified inbox with priority scoring across all properties, (2) operational context overlay enriching every message with PMS data, (3) auto-resolution for known questions, (4) cross-property pattern detection, and (5) report integration surfacing financial, occupancy, and maintenance data as conversational context.

This means AutoProxy does not replace Slack, Teams, or Email. It ingests from multiple channels, enriches with operational data from the PMS, routes/filters/summarizes/auto-responds, and presents a unified view per user role. The executive sees only exception-based alerts and decision requests. The regional manager sees cross-property patterns and anomalies. The GM sees prioritized operational messages.

This is significantly more ambitious than the on-site "personal secretary" concept from the original vision. It is also significantly more defensible. A messaging app is a commodity. A communication intelligence layer that understands both operational context (this property's occupancy is at 95%, maintenance has 3 open tickets) and communication context (the regional manager just asked about weekend staffing) is a platform.

The risk is scope creep. The single-pane vision is compelling but requires multi-channel integration, which is Phase 3 of the corporate build. Starting with report intelligence (Phase 1) and cross-property routing (Phase 2) builds toward the vision incrementally rather than attempting it all at once.
